text.title: Django Decomposition Layer Rules
text.summary: Service/selector/model/view rules, file-size thresholds, and layer boundary enforcement with import-linter.

rule.statement: Organize Django apps into four layers: models/ (ORM fields + simple validators + custom managers, no business logic), selectors/ (read-only QuerySet functions, no mutations), services/ (write operations with @transaction.atomic, keyword-only args), views/ (parse request, call service/selector, serialize response — 10-20 lines per method). Never mix layers in one file.
rule.rationale: Fat views mix HTTP, business logic, and DB queries, making each layer untestable in isolation. Fat models create hidden coupling between data and behavior. The four-layer split produces single-purpose, independently testable units.

section.title: Service Function Rules

rule.statement: Service functions use keyword-only arguments: def user_create(*, email: str, password: str) -> User. Decorate with @transaction.atomic only when composing multiple writes. Never accept HTTP request objects or return HTTP responses.
rule.rationale: Positional args drift silently as signatures evolve — a reorder breaks callers without a type error. Keyword-only args make the interface explicit. @transaction.atomic on every service is wasteful and wraps single-write ops in a no-op that misleads readers.

section.reference: Full UserService with dataclass inputs and keyword-only args

section.title: Selector Rules

rule.statement: Selectors are pure functions returning QuerySet or typed iterables. They must not call save(), update(), delete(), or any service. They must include select_related/prefetch_related for all accessed related objects. Return type must be annotated.
rule.rationale: Untypeable selectors that sometimes return QuerySets and sometimes evaluated lists make N+1 detection impossible. A caller that evaluates a queryset inside a loop needs to know it is a queryset.

section.reference: User selectors with typed returns and eager loading

rule.statement: Trigger decomposition when: models.py exceeds 300 lines → split to models/ package; services/ file exceeds 200 lines → split by subdomain; views.py exceeds 200 lines → split to views/ package. Add db_table = 'original_name' to model Meta when splitting models.py to avoid migration.
rule.rationale: File-size thresholds are objective triggers that prevent "I'll refactor later" drift. The db_table Meta attribute makes Django treat the split-package models as the same DB table, avoiding a zero-downtime migration.

section.title: Layer Boundary Enforcement

rule.statement: Use import-linter to enforce that services cannot import views or serializers, and that models cannot import services. Add the .importlinter config to the repo root and run it in CI on every PR.
rule.rationale: Without enforcement, layers re-couple within weeks. An agent that adds a service.call_view() import destroys the boundary silently. import-linter makes violations a CI failure.

section.reference: import-linter config enforcing the four-layer boundary
